title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
An adult reel just popped up...! My boyfriend's dad's face now... "Is this your dad by any chance?" They might send something like that to you... "He's the..." "He's the adult content guy." Then the dad is watching it like this... Since it's floating up there. That's what I'm watching. Just die! Ugh, I'm really upset. I seriously need to die. I can't do this. Uh, no, what's wrong? - Ugh, my head hurts.
- No, it's fine. She looks like she might really cry today! No, no. You're so emotional. Have you seen it lately? Seen the views?
It's insane, right? Eyes down, you jerk. Hey! Come out and treat me like royalty. Hey, hey, he's coming again
gonna brag about his watch again.

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
he might really cry today! No, no. You're so emotional. Have you seen it lately? Seen the views?
It's insane, right? Eyes down, you jerk. Hey! Come out and treat me like royalty. Hey, hey, he's coming again
gonna brag about his watch again. From the moment he walks in, just like... That's cute. - Oh, so cute.
- Hey, that's cute! Wow, it's payday today. The moving guys with their trucks like... Ugh, you... Hopeless. Before the others get here
hurry up and explain that ring... Explain... explain it now. What ring...? What are you doing? I hate you, bro. What? Now, please look forward to what's coming. You're hopeless. Hehehe. You're hopeless! Wow... Isn't the reaction a bit too extreme? Oh... we need to introduce ourselves because it's not just one person
two showed up at the same time! We do our introductions ourselves, okay? I'm Heo Mi Jin, the "T" girl who goes hunting with Ji You. Nice to meet you! You're a "T"? Are you f***ing "T"? I'm Han Ji Won Ji You's little sister and a member of the Fox Club with her. Nice to meet you. Ji You couldn't make it today so she sent two of you instead. Nice to meet you... You seem super awkward. Sometimes, Horse King might just put his hands over in the middle like this. Don't take it the wrong way. Can I do it too? - Oh, of course.
- Anything is fine. Oh really? Someone you know can instantly 
become your partner. So first, we need to do a little background check. Mi Jin and Ji Won you're on the
"Friends with the opposite sex exist" side, right? Honestly, I'm on the "They don't exist" side. You are? Yeah, as I get older
I'm moving to the "Don't exist" side… - Right!
- Exactly! They just disappear over time. Man, without Ji You here it's a lonely battle today. So, to help you all feel more at ease we thought we'd fill up a bit
before getting started. Mind if we start eating? - Oh, this bread is made with Seoul Milk?
- It's bread. Yes, it's Seoul Milk bread. It's made using
premium ingredients from Seoul Milk. For the milk cream bread it uses fresh cream from Seoul Milk
so the cream is rich and fresh. It contains over 60% animal-based cream. That's a good thing. Animal cream is made from milk fat while plant-based cream is a processed substitute. So naturally
the higher the animal cream content the richer and smoother it tastes, right? The milk cream castella, of course is also packed with Seoul Milk's fresh cream so it's filled with fresh cream inside. Even the bread is made with Seoul Milk so it's

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
higher the animal cream content the richer and smoother it tastes, right? The milk cream castella, of course is also packed with Seoul Milk's fresh cream so it's filled with fresh cream inside. Even the bread is made with Seoul Milk so it's moist and soft. For the milk cream donut... Sounds delicious, haha. Now, for the milk cream donut you know Seoul Milk's condensed milk? It's in there too making it soft and sweet. Oh, there's this in here too. Oh, you're supposed to scoop it out? That's a lot of cream! The milk cream bread is loaded with cream too! Seriously packed. So much, it's crazy. - It's super rich! Super fresh!
- There's so much cream… This castella is really good. Mmm. This castella is so moist so soft and fluffy. It's my favorite kind of bread. These kinds of breads are really trendy right now. - It's really delicious!
- Mmm! Maybe because of the condensed milk inside
it's really sweet. Wait, but donuts are usually supposed to have a hole in the middle, right? Maybe they thought people
would complain if the hole was empty so they just filled it all up. They stuffed it with cream. Totally my kind of thing. The bread itself is super moist. - It's rich, it's rich.
- Right? Oh, it got on me…

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
, right? Maybe they thought people
would complain if the hole was empty so they just filled it all up. They stuffed it with cream. Totally my kind of thing. The bread itself is super moist. - It's rich, it's rich.
- Right? Oh, it got on me… ugh… I, uh... when I was preparing for 
a bodybuilding competition the one thing I craved the most was donuts. But I've never seen one
with this much cream before. It's packed like this much inside. Hey, but Horse King,
when you're talking... can you please look side to side a bit? Ugh, I can't do this anymore. I'm... I, uh... This is weird. There's so much cream 
that you could even flirt with it. Hehehe. But Mi Jin must've foamed at the mouth
because it was so good. What would I do... If you gave your partner a bite of your bread and they ate all the parts with the cream. Would you be annoyed or not? It's your partner. I wouldn't be… wouldn't I not be? - Oh, you wouldn't?
- You would? - You wouldn't be annoyed?
- Nope, I wouldn't. Why? Because she's my girlfriend. - Me neither.
- See? Told you! I don't think I'd get annoyed either. For me... I think it comes down to consideration. And consideration ties directly into love. So then I'd start thinking
"This means they don't love me enough." It just spirals like that. You're such a draining type. At the start of a relationship
I don't say anything. I hold it in. But once we're close... then I get annoyed. That's why we need this. Exactly. There's so much cream. Yeah, you can share it. There's too much cream to finish alone. Even the balance game today was a perfect tie-in for the PPL selling points. Actually, I contacted Ji You yesterday. What? Oh, a female friend! I even reached out to hyung, for work stuff. - You called me.
- But she didn't answer! Why didn't she answer? - Ah!
- She was abroad. - Overseas, right!
- That's right, that's right! Ah, that's why I texted. So I texted and then... Sigh... Why are we even talking about this? Huh? - Don't be nervous!
- Ugh, what's with you? This is driving me crazy. What's going on? I feel like I'm the guest here. Seriously, hopeless. The topic is hopeless, lol. Sometimes the person is hopeless and sometimes it's the situation. You had a creepy nightmare while sleeping woke up screaming
and your boyfriend woke up to the noise and said "What the...!" What would you do? Ah... The girlfriend woke up from a nightmare and she woke up with a creepy face. So it wasn't the scream
but her face that made you go, "What

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
e up screaming
and your boyfriend woke up to the noise and said "What the...!" What would you do? Ah... The girlfriend woke up from a nightmare and she woke up with a creepy face. So it wasn't the scream
but her face that made you go, "What the ...!" Like when you have such a terrifying dream sometimes you wake up like this, you know? Uugh... If it's like that, then he's just startled. You know how some people are super sensitive? Like, "What the..." With that deep frown. That could really hurt your feelings. When someone says "What the..." like that... No matter the situation it makes them seem kind of off. Like, just saying "What the..." makes you wonder
how they might act when they're actually angry. But I feel like… if someone's really sweet, they wouldn't say
"What the..." even if they were woken up. What about a sweet "What the..."? How would you do it? I see you. If you do it like this, I like it. I see you... - I am watching you.
- Waking up from sleep… You know what "What the..." really is? It's just that the "f***" part
is short for the full curse. Like, "What the f***." Oh, that would really hurt. I'd cry if that happened. Ji Won, so you're actually a lot more
sensitive than I thought, huh? If I heard that I'd honestly turn away and tear up. And you can't even show that you're crying, huh? - Yes...!
- Wow...! If, by any chance
we go a little overboard trying to be funny today and it hurts your feelings please turn around and cry where I can't see you. - I've still got a job to do.
- Got it... Should I do that too? Would that make me seem more feminine? No, no.
You should just go with your own style. Mi Jin, what's your style? For me, waking up screaming means you're suddenly asleep and then "Aah!" Wouldn't that be shocking? Yeah, of course... And then going, "What the..."
Isn't that kind of understandable? Ah, that's it!
Now that Mi Jin said it, I get it. Normally, when someone's startled they'd go, "Whoa! What's wrong? Are you okay?" "What happened? What's going on?" That's how you'd react
but instead, they skip all of that and go "Ack! What the..." Hahaha... that's so scary... It's not about checking on the other person it's more like "You startled me and made me uncomfortable."
that kind of vibe. They put their own emotions first. I'm really curious about something. Since both of you are here, I wanted to ask when you dream about your boyfriend
cheating on you... a lot of people actually get mad, right? I guess that's how

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
d of vibe. They put their own emotions first. I'm really curious about something. Since both of you are here, I wanted to ask when you dream about your boyfriend
cheating on you... a lot of people actually get mad, right? I guess that's how they see it. I don't know why that happens. But what about you two? It's because the emotions still linger. I know in my head it's not real but I haven't shaken off the feelings
from the dream yet. So without realizing it those thoughts keep coming back... and I can't help but get a little upset. - Look, she said she can't help but get upset.
- But I don't get that at all. - Yeah, I don't get it either.
- Didn't you just say you know it's not real? But my heart doesn't know that, okay? Then just imagine
how dumbfounded the boyfriend must be. Like, "What did I even do?" Getting sulky over something
that happened in a dream while sleeping... Still, in the dream, he did cheat and I woke up with all those emotions still there. What the... That's when the "What the..." comes out. She looks like she might really cry today! - No way.
- You're so emotional. Ji Won, are you a "F"? Totally "F." I'm a "T." Total "T," total "F." Then, Ji Won who's fully immersed in her feelings why don't you read us
the first balance game question? Your partner is getting relationship advice
which situation is worse? Asking the internet community about everything vs.
asking their opposite-sex friend about everything. They both suck so bad! Oh, by "friend," you mean... - guy friend or girl friend? Like, the opposite sex?
- Yes. You mean female or male friend? Doesn't that just not make sense? Wait, isn't a "friend" like that
supposed to mean someone of the same sex you know? Now you're just twisting the definition
to fit your own feelings, huh? Ah, I didn't know that. Ah… this is gonna turn into a lonely battle again. Asking the internet community about everything that's honestly really bad too, right? It's super bad. Seriously, it's like they live on those forums. The thing about
online communities is there's no filter. It's just a bunch of different opinions and once you see them
you can't help but have them stick in your head. But an opposite-sex friend
will just take their side like "Yeah, you did the right thing." "It's their fault." That's what happens. An opposite-sex friend - is going to take their side!
- Exactly, exactly. And they're the opposite sex! Then one thing leads to another, and
they might even end up touching

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
you did the right thing." "It's their fault." That's what happens. An opposite-sex friend - is going to take their side!
- Exactly, exactly. And they're the opposite sex! Then one thing leads to another, and
they might even end up touching or something. - Huh?
- You never know what could happen! Why would that suddenly lead to physical contact? It does happen. No, I'm telling you, it happens! Why would guy and girl friends
suddenly get physical? Today, seriously…
this is such a lonely battle for me! At this point, I've got no one on my side. None. Not even one person. Look, even you're starting to understand the concept of guy-girl friendships a little now. - Yes!
- Ji You! - Yea... wait what?
- Like Ji You! Do we have to do this again? How many weeks have we been doing this now...? We're almost at episode 30... Why can't I get out of this loop...? It's because 
men and women feel things differently! That's why we ask!
Like asking a female friend "I got upset over this, but
I'm not sure exactly which part triggered me..." But the girlfriend won't say anything. Just sulks, "Hmph! Whatever!" But? I don't know what I did wrong! So you go ask a guy friend and they're like, "You dumb***." Go ask your guy friends - that's all guys ever say!
- Right. No, listen to me seriously for a sec... "Hmm... really?" "You dumb***!" Yeah, that's just how we do it. Now I'm the frustrated one. I need to ask someone too! So I go to Ji You and say "Hey, she got upset about this and this
where exactly did I go wrong?" I'm in the "I can ask" camp, okay! Like earlier
she's super in touch with her feelings, right? So let's say she comes over
and starts crying like that... Are you just gonna sit there like this? No, you'd say, "It's okay, it's okay." Right. And then boom
next thing you know, you're kissing. Who even does that? Why do you like that...? What is wrong with you… seriously... This place... is no longer a space for rational discussion. If you say, "She was upset..." and ask, "Which part do you think it was?" girls actually get it! They'll say, "Work through that part." And you'll get a real answer! - A real answer?
- Yes! "Hmm, now that you gave me the answer" "wanna seal it with a kiss?" That's not what we're talking about, you know? No! Who the hell would even do that? This whole relationship advice thing it's super emotional. If your guy or girl friend is comforting you and happens to be attractive... Yeah, that happens a lot! - Exactly!
- You fight

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
g about, you know? No! Who the hell would even do that? This whole relationship advice thing it's super emotional. If your guy or girl friend is comforting you and happens to be attractive... Yeah, that happens a lot! - Exactly!
- You fight with your partner, break up and end up dating the friend
who was listening to your problems. Right? People literally start dating
during "relationship talks"! That's why it's a no-go! Wait, hold on, are we... C... could you not glare at me like that? That's why it's not okay! Make your choice. I pick the community. I pick the community too. I pick the community too. I pick the friend. Me too, friend. Wait, what? Suddenly? - Wait, Ji Won's doing it now?
- Pink, pink! If he's got a girl friend, that's a no-go. When my boyfriend tells his girl friend about me does that make me more upset? Or when he posts about me on an online forum and I read the post… and the comments! Which one feels worse? That's how I think about it. When it comes to this I feel like there would be
tons of unfiltered comments online! Exactly. Stuff like, "Just break up." Or, "Your girlfriend's dumb." Seeing things like that would just… really tick me off. And honestly, I hate that my girlfriend would
entrust our important relationship issue to random strangers writing those comments! - Right?
- It just feels so careless. That's what I hate about it.
Think about it, hyung... Asking a friend for advice? That's normal. That's human. Yeah, and to add onto what Ji Won said this person is already - completely immersed in that online community.
- Exactly. Like they're constantly asking for advice to the point
they're even posting about their love life they just seem like someone
who can't think for themselves. Right...! In this case? They're just asking one friend. Because they like them so! so so much and they don't know what to do
so they keep asking for advice. But then again
in your head, Horse King everything always ends with a kiss, no matter what. No, that's not it! So you might prefer
to just go kiss the computer screen. Now look, just look! The thing is with online communities, you can choose to stop. - If you don't post, it ends right there!
- Whoa, exactly! Of course! But let's say I meet up with a friend
and their face looks off. Then you naturally ask, "Is something wrong?" and start exchanging feelings. Are you saying you can't end
an opposite-sex friendship on your own terms? Of course you can't. You could bump into them
at

title: 연애할 때 걸러야 하는 유형ㅣ영업중EP.31 [EN]
text:
with a friend
and their face looks off. Then you naturally ask, "Is something wrong?" and start exchanging feelings. Are you saying you can't end
an opposite-sex friendship on your own terms? Of course you can't. You could bump into them
at a work dinner or something... See that they're upset and go, "What's wrong? Really?" And you're having a full-on relationship talk. But with a community
once you decide to stop using it, it's over. Let's say they've decided
not to do any of that anymore... I mean, I used to be a hot icon
on the forums, okay? Yeah, he's the king of online forums. I've browsed all kinds of communities. And honestly, most of the posts are fake. If we just don't read the comments, we're fine! But how could you not read them? The whole point of posting is for the comments! What are you even saying? Let's say I'm the one being posted about if my girlfriend wrote about me I'd just ignore the comments and think "Oh, she just needed advice." Ugh, here we go again. Here comes the "so cool" syndrome again. Imagine this she talked to her guy friend
about our bedroom issues. - Wow!
- Right. I'd be way more pissed
if she posted that on the community! Like, imagine this "My boyfriend can't last ten minutes." Ugh... "three-minute curry." Then it's funny! What's funny about that? It's just... kind of funny! But now imagine she tells her guy friend "My boyfriend can't even last three minutes." Imagine she says that. He's like,
"Oh really? I can last more than three." - That kind of conversation...
- Ack! Hahahahaha. Okay, that was an extreme example. I'm joking! Just joking! "I can last more than three..." Hehehe. I mean I could if I wanted to. - I mean, just joking!
- You're insane! Come on, not really being serious. It's just playful banter between friends. Do you think 
saying it playfully somehow makes it better? "I can go more than three..." But if it's someone you really love even if your friend's hearing you out they're probably
not gonna start trash-talking that person, right? But if people on a forum start mocking the person I love like that
I'd be pissed. Now imagine my boyfriend
was doing that behind my back I'd be furious. Right? It turns into public shaming. But that's the thing
these communities are anonymous. Because they're anonymous
people feel free to go all in with the roasting. Exactly! I've got something embarrassing. Let's say I've got a huge mole on my butt. But no one knows it's me, right? Then I see a post like "Yo… even posted about the mole on my butt?" And the comments are like,
talking about Bamseom or something... "Bamseom Butt." - "Butt-seom," lol.
- Black hole… Comments about black hole and also a choco bun. Okay, fine what if the two of them meet up and say "Look, my boyfriend has a giant mole, see?" That's what makes it a concern
but also way too extreme. I mean, no one actually shows
their partner's mole to a friend, right? Is that really the concern? That truly doesn't make sense. Having a mole on your partner's butt
is your big concern? It could be.
It could be something that turns you off. True, it could happen. Like if you're already starting
to lose feelings a bit that kind of thing becomes a concern. But not like
"Ah, there's a mole on her butt…" "It's 6cm in diameter." Wow, so detailed. That's the kind of thing that's not a real issue. Let's just think in normal terms. Normally... You don't get roasted like that. Your post might not even get any views. Not everything becomes a best post or blows up as a hot topic. But that actually makes
online communities even worse! You know why? You posted a few times like "Having trouble communicating with my boyfriend." Six views. Hmm… maybe I should spice it up a bit? - Hahaha.
- No way, lol. "Having trouble communicating" "during s** with my boyfriend." Boom, 600 views. Wait a sec... "My boyfriend wants to talk" "in the middle of s** time." Lol. - And then…
- This is... That's even better! It's basically fiction. It's basically fiction, isn't it? It's fantasy! I used to post on
Naver Knowledge iN back in middle school. - I used to write stuff too!
- Right. And you'd get responses
from "lawyers" and "doctors." "Doctor," lol. What kind of problem was it? Well... it was out of sexual curiosity. - Hahahaha.
- So I asked on Knowledge iN. He probably got points for that post. That doctor. Yeah, I gave them points back then. That's how it was. They totally farmed your points, hahaha. Like one time, I had a cough "I'm coughing a lot lately, what should I do?" I posted that online. Next thing I know I've apparently got an incurable disease. From reading all the comments by people who aren't even doctors all those unfiltered replies made me think "Wait… is it really that serious?" "Do I really have that disease?" Just like that, those posts start sticking in your head. Like, "Maybe my boyfriend really is the problem?" Even if it's not true
you can't help but start believing it. Let me just add one thing to that. Lately, because I've been doing that soccer show my face has gotten a bit darker, right? On a YouTube video, someone commented "Why is Kwak Beom so tan now?" And someone else seriously replied "He's been out
in the sun filming a soccer program." Then another said
"No, he's just been drinking a lot lately." And that's where it started, things escalated. Next comment
"I heard his liver isn't doing well." Then
"Apparently he has cirrhosis." And finally
"I heard he passed away." See? That's how it goes! If it ended there, fine. But someone actually took that and made a YouTube Shorts. What, really? They made a video titled
"Breaking: Kwak Beom dies of liver cancer." What the hell? And my wife saw it. Now, I could just laugh it off like "Eh, whatever, they're just messing around." But she was really upset. Of course, that'd upset your family. The last comment on that thread was
"His family is grieving." Like, what the hell? Are they insane? That's why I hate this stuff. Those forums, communities... I hold a bit of resentment toward them. And remember, Horse King?
At our company dinner you said it yourself "I never read the comments." Yeah, I don't read them! Why don't you read them? Because once it's out there
it's not really me anymore. Whatever they're saying over there - Great job...
- even if there are nice comments most of it is just garbage, insults or just plain hate. Right! - Useless stuff all over the place.
- Exactly! - Well done!
- You don't even care about all this internet stuff. If so, would you want to be with someone who depends on that
for answers to their life problems? Whoa... that was so well said. Wow, you nailed it. Wait, hold on a sec... See that, you idiot? Did you catch that? "Three-minute curry" guy? That's exactly it! Like if I tell pH-1 "Hey, I kinda nicked my pinky." and that story keeps spreading I'll end up being
the guy who ate his own pinky. That's the vicious cycle of online forums. And like we talked about earlier if dreams can mess with your emotions that much then opposite-sex friends? When the advice comes from someone
who truly cares about me they'll say stuff like "Try again with them." "You can work it out." "Maybe bring it up this way." You get real, heartfelt advice. But forums? That's not what you get. And when someone tries to solve problems within their own circle
I think that's really beautiful. So now you've really shifted
to the guy friend, girl friend camp, huh? They're both hopeless, honestly. But we’ve all agreed 
that the online forums are worse. And no one's been convinced today, right? No one from our side switched to theirs either? Not a single person changed sides today. Like a citizen's vote We should each go down 
and take part at least once. We should really go around with a board hit up the producers around Sangam-dong. and have people put stickers on it. - Seriously though!
- For real. There are a lot of comments like
"I want to join in too!" Split into teams like debate squads. Let's throw down on Christmas Eve at Yeouido Park. Guy and girl friends exist team
vs. don't exist team. Let's settle it once and for all! Let's have a showdown. Take a bite and move to the next round. We usually don't get this fired up
right from the start. - Tough one, huh?
- Yeah, this was hard. Yeah, this one was a bit tough. Who's more annoying when settling up? Someone who pays less than they owe vs.
Someone who gives you slightly more expensive goods in return. Like they owe you 10,000 won
but bring you 12,000 won worth of Korean melon or sweet potatoes... That's a hard one. Do people really do that? Like, you go out to eat, split the bill and they're like, "I'll pay you next time." You thought they'd just send the money
but next time, they show up with one Korean melon. One Korean melon that costs about 13,000 won. Or we split the bill but next time they showed up with a can of tuna, and some Spam like, "Hey, this is for last time…" But technically, the second type did give you something of equal value. Still, if the first type keeps shorting you that gets really annoying. Let's vote, who's more annoying? I pick the first one. - Me too.
- Same here. I'm picking the second. Me too. Man, I'm the odd one out again today... But hey, it's fun. When introducing your friend tell them
"Next time they'll pay you back in fruit." Or, "Hey, that guy over there…
he's gonna underpay, okay?" "Just wanted to let you know." - That's even weirder!
- And also... I've figured out exactly how to convert Beom. From now on, let's pay only his appearance fee in goods. Actually no, it should be the opposite. Yeah, the opposite. From now on just underpay Beom a few bucks every time. I'm okay with that. Use the difference to buy some goods. "Mr. Beom, your appearance fee has arrived." "Huh? What appearance fee?" And then Seoul Milk bread appears. "Thank you for your hard work, Mr. Beom." Ah, I self-converted. Why are you being converted? Let's stack up four weeks' worth
and ship it all together. "Hello, is this Mr. Kwak Beom from ** Apartments?" "Your appearance fee has arrived." Huh? What are you talking about? "Could you come down to B1?" The moving guys with their trucks like... Two delivery guys hop out like... Wajajaajajaja... "Where should we put these?" Hey pH-1, thanks, man. I changed to the second option. I hate this. But technically that was your appearance fee whereas the other was just friends splitting bill. Still, when it's friends
you might be more understanding. Like, "Ah… they must be going through something." When someone sends pricey goods it kind of feels like a surprise box. Like, "Hmm… what are they gonna send this time?" Honestly, even for appearance fees random truck deliveries could hit 
that dopamine spot. "Whoa! It's payday today!" Yay! But for me, that act itself annoys me. Like when someone tries to even it out with kimchi but doesn't even match the value. Going to the market in the morning like "Uh... 21,000 won...
add 3,000 won more…" Checking on Coupang for Jonggatjip kimchi pricing. How much per gram. Hey, Jonggatjip kimchi's expensive, okay? But my family's kimchi has more salted seafood so I reduced the portion but it's about the same value. But in some situations you just can't argue with it. "My mom..." "actually grew this corn herself…" - That's right...
- But what if I don't eat corn some people might not like corn. There are people who don't like corn...? Yeah, some people don't. You can dislike it
but the thought still gets across. But when someone underpays money is sensitive. Like, if we all paid 10,000 won and you only sent 8,000 won. Then I'm like… "Should I say something?" Suddenly I'm the petty one, over 2,000 won. I ate 2,000 won less worth of food! I didn't eat the crust! Yeah, I didn't eat the crust. Then next time they do the same thing again… Now you're like, "Should I say something?" Hmm, this is getting annoying… - And now you're overthinking it.
- All over 2,000 won. I'm picking the first. - Right?
- No way around it. But let's say you didn't send the cash 
and later text "Yo, I'll make it up to you." The doorbell rings and suddenly you are like "Hey, remember that 10,000 won from last time?" "Here's a Korean melon. Enjoy." "This is worth around 13,000 won." "Bro, let's eat it together! Come on in!" "Nah, it's okay." But because it's a Korean melon
